title: semele androgyna
intro: Semele androgyna, also known as the male fern or androgynous semele, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Osmundaceae. This plant is native to Asia and Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of Semele androgyna have been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Semele androgyna can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
